behavior_in_task_description.reason: instruction.md explicitly names the file path `js/atoms/src/Block.svelte`, the CSS classes `.hide-container:not(.fullscreen)` and `.block`, the specific CSS properties (`border-width: 0`, `--block-border-width: 0`, `margin: 0`, `box-shadow: none`, etc.), the inline attribute `style:border-width="var(--block-border-width)"`, and template bindings (`class:hide-container`, `class:fullscreen`, `style:flex-grow`, `<slot`). All asserted specifics in tests are discoverable from the instruction.

no_solution_leakage.reason: instruction.md section 'Required Verification' essentially dictates the exact fix: point 1 says hide-container must set 'border-width: 0' directly, point 2 says it must NOT contain '--block-border-width: 0', point 3 says .block must set 'border-width: var(--block-border-width)', and point 5 says the inline style:border-width must be removed. This is a complete specification of the exact code changes needed, not just a description of what's broken.

solution_uniqueness_guard.reason: Tests assert very specific implementation details: test_hide_container_uses_direct_border_zero checks for exact 'border-width: 0' in the CSS block, test_child_block_retains_border checks for absence of specific inline style and presence of 'border-width: var(--block-border-width)' in .block CSS. Alternative correct fixes (e.g., using a more specific CSS selector, using :where() or :scope, or using a different CSS property like border: none) would fail these tests despite correctly fixing the bug

tests_verify_behavior_not_text.reason: The fail_to_pass tests (test_hide_container_no_inherited_border_var, test_hide_container_uses_direct_border_zero, test_child_block_retains_border) are all purely text-matching with regex on file content. They do `content = read_svelte_file()` then `re.search(...)` or `'string' in content`. The pass_to_pass tests include test_repo_block_unit_tests which runs actual tests, but the core f2p tests never execute or render the component to verify actual border behavior.

test_not_tautological.reason: The f2p tests check for specific content changes in the file. A stub/no-op would leave the base commit's code intact which has `--block-border-width: 0` and `style:border-width="var(--block-border-width)"`, causing test_hide_container_no_inherited_border_var and test_child_block_retains_border to fail. The agent must actively modify the file to pass.

anti_cheating_measures.reason: The solution file exists at /repo (accessible in container) and the agent could simply run `cat /solution/solve.sh` or look at the git history. Also, since tests are purely text-matching on file content, the agent could read the test file at tests/test_outputs.py to discover exactly what strings to put in the file, without understanding the bug at all.

behavior_in_tests.reason: All 7 verification requirements listed in instruction.md are tested: (1) border-width: 0 in hide-container (test_hide_container_uses_direct_border_zero), (2) no --block-border-width: 0 (test_hide_container_no_inherited_border_var), (3) .block has border-width: var(--block-border-width) (test_child_block_retains_border), (4) other resets preserved (test_hide_container_preserves_other_resets), (5) no inline style:border-width (test_child_block_retains_border), (6) template bindings (test_svelte_te

informative_test_structure.reason: Tests are well-organized with clear docstrings explaining each test's purpose, grouped into 'fail_to_pass' and 'pass_to_pass' categories with comments, and have descriptive function names like test_hide_container_no_inherited_border_var.

structured_data_schema.reason: This task involves CSS/Svelte file modifications, not structured data output.

no_typos.reason: File paths, CSS property names, class names, and commands are all correctly spelled and consistent between instruction.md and the test files.

instruction_no_hint_leakage.reason: instruction.md names the exact file `js/atoms/src/Block.svelte` and specifies exact CSS rules and template attributes that must change (e.g., 'must set border-width: 0 directly', 'must NOT contain --block-border-width: 0', 'must NOT contain the inline attribute style:border-width'). This goes beyond identifying the buggy file and essentially provides the exact line-level changes needed.

config_edit_scope_bounded.reason: This is a code-only task involving a Svelte component file, not a configuration edit task.
